mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 320, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy. This sustained high real rates compress equity valuations and drive 10-year Treasury yields upward as market participants price in prolonged rate persistence. The mechanism operates continuously when both conditions hold.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.22% and CPIAUCSL at 323.364 near trigger threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in more than 55% of 20-day forward periods when both FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 320, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk. This triggers flight-to-safety demand for long-duration Treasuries, driving 10-year yields downward as investors reduce equity exposure and reallocate to fixed income.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.3%, near trigger level
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in more than 50% of 20-day forward periods when UNRATE > 4.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and yield changes is positive (opposite of predicted direction) over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both decline month-over-month by more than 2%, growth expectations compress, reducing inflation expectations and driving 10-year Treasury yields lower as the market reprices growth and inflation risk downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% MoM AND HOUST declines > 2% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 732010.0M and HOUST at 1307.0K
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in more than 55% of 20-day forward periods when both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. This uncertainty increases term premium on long-duration Treasuries, driving 10-year yields upward as investors demand compensation for inflation and growth uncertainty.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.9203, recent trend shows modest growth
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in more than 50% of 5-day forward periods when INDPRO MoM growth < 0.5%, or the mechanism fails to predict yield direction with Rank IC > 0.06 over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month, indicating labor market cooling. This signals the Fed may pause or cut rates within 2-3 months, reducing long-term rate expectations and driving 10-year Treasury yields downward as the market reprices terminal rate lower.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 159540K, recent monthly changes indicate labor market dynamics
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in more than 55% of 60-day forward periods when PAYEMS MoM growth < 100K, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 1.5% annualized, combined with CPIAUCSL inflation remaining above 315. This stagflation signal increases equity risk premium and long-duration bond term premium simultaneously, driving 10-year Treasury yields upward as investors demand higher compensation for both growth and inflation risk.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 23770.976B and CPIAUCSL at 323.364
regime_hint: stagflation or late-cycle expansion with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in more than 55% of 60-day forward periods when both GDPC1 growth < 1.5% and CPIAUCSL > 315, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling monetary easing. This rate cut cycle reduces discount rates on future equity cash flows and lowers long-term rate expectations, driving 10-year Treasury yields downward as the market reprices growth and monetary accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.22%, recent trend shows potential for easing
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or recession prevention
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in more than 55% of 20-day forward periods when FEDFUNDS declines > 0.5pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1400K annualized units while retail sales (RSAFS) grow above 2% MoM, signaling robust consumer demand and housing activity. This demand strength increases inflation expectations and reduces recession risk, driving 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market reprices growth and inflation higher.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1307.0K and RSAFS at 732010.0M
regime_hint: strong demand expansion or late-cycle acceleration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in more than 55% of 20-day forward periods when both HOUST > 1400K and RSAFS MoM growth > 2%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.
